strengths: 15+ years across full-stack web, with recent TypeScript/Node.js and React experience matching the primary stack
Hands-on AI-agent and MCP tooling work at re:cinq, directly relevant to the AI agent development requirement
Strong testing and craftsmanship background (TDD, Vitest-era practices, authored test runner and assertion libraries, taught Selenium/TDD courses)
concerns: No explicit Nest.js experience mentioned in the resume
Limited evidence of Kotlin and only general Java exposure for the secondary backend stack
Cloud platform breadth shown via AWS CDK only; no explicit GCP/Azure or Kubernetes-in-production detail beyond GISCollective
response.justification: The candidate brings well over the required 6 years of fullstack experience, with a recent and directly relevant role building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling in TypeScript and Node.js, plus solid React, MongoDB, AWS, and Docker exposure. Engineering-craft signals are strong: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source test tooling, and teaching. Gaps are around the specific Nest.js framework and limited Kotlin depth, though the polyglot background suggests these are learnable rather than blocking.
